title: vaccinium × hagerupii
intro: Vaccinium × hagerupii, also known as the Hagerup blueberry, is a hybrid plant species in the heath family (Ericaceae) and is a cross between Vaccinium myrtillus and Vaccinium uliginosum. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Vaccinium × hagerupii are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and other polyphenols, which are believed to help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, and may also have potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular benefits. Vaccinium × hagerupii can be prepared into herbal teas, extracts, and supplements, but more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
